design_philosophy: A clear, functional, and highly visual sports analytics platform. Driven by precision, high contrast, and energetic athletic accents. Avoids generic AI 'slop' aesthetics, specifically banning purple/violet gradients and standard fonts like Inter/Roboto. Designed to feel premium, sporty, and competitive against top-tier apps like Hudl and Volleymetrics.

headings.usage: Use for all headlines, card titles, dashboard metrics, and key emphasis. Communicates sporty precision.

body.usage: Use for dense data tables, dashboard text, datavolley logging buttons, and general UI text. Highly readable for analytics.

layout_and_spacing.philosophy: Swiss Grid structure. High density for the datavolley/analytics views, generous spacing for marketing/login views.
layout_and_spacing.dashboard_grid: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 or gap-6
layout_and_spacing.bento_grid: Grid children must use col-span/row-span effectively. Use h-full to stretch items to match neighbors.
layout_and_spacing.surfaces: Use flat surfaces (bg-white) with crisp 1px borders (border-slate-200). Use shadow-sm for active states, otherwise prefer flat/no-shadow design for a clean, pro-sports feel.

components.buttons: Shadcn Button customized with rounded-md (not heavily pill-shaped). Primary buttons use brand Orange. Secondary use brand Deep Blue or outline.
components.cards: Cardless aesthetic where possible, or extremely flat cards. No default heavy shadows. Use border-slate-200.
components.navigation: Left sidebar for dashboards with subtle active states (bg-slate-100 text-orange-600). Sticky top header with glassmorphism (backdrop-blur-md bg-white/80 border-b border-slate-200).
components.datavolley_tracker: Large, highly-tappable buttons for intermediate mode. Clearly color-coded (Green for Kill/Ace, Red for Error) to prevent misclicks during live action.
components.court_visualization: Hero component. Use a 2D top-down isometric or flat pure SVG court. The court lines should be bright white or orange over a distinct solid background (e.g., deep blue or light wood). Players must be draggable distinct nodes with clear numbers.

motion.interactions: Snappy, high-performance transitions. transition-all duration-200 ease-in-out for buttons/cards.
motion.data_updates: When stats update, use a subtle background flash (e.g., flash light green for a positive stat update) to indicate live recording.

icons.usage: Use Duotone or Regular weights. Essential for representing complex sports analytics data clearly.

hero_court_background.description: Minimalist vector illustration of a volleyball court with glowing tactical lines. Use for landing page hero, login screen background, or tactical board backdrop.

sports_motion_abstract.description: Dynamic white/gray/orange motion abstract background. Use for onboarding flows or empty states to add visual interest.

match_action_1.description: Men playing volleyball. Great for the multimedia gallery or generic match placeholders.

match_action_2.description: Outdoor court volleyball action. Use as fallback image for team profiles or match results.

coach_tablet.description: Close up of a person holding a tablet. Excellent contextual image for marketing the datavolley coach features.

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure all text, especially within data visualization and on the court UI, meets APCA/WCAG AA contrast ratios (e.g., White text on Deep Blue or Orange, Slate 900 text on Light backgrounds).
accessibility_and_testing.testing_attributes: All interactive elements, forms, datavolley buttons, and navigation MUST include a `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="datavolley-btn-ace"`, `data-testid="nav-roster"`).

instructions_to_main_agent: Install Google Fonts 'Outfit' and 'Manrope' in the frontend.
Implement JWT + Google Auth login on the landing/auth pages.
Build a role-based layout: Left sidebar for navigation with options dynamically shown based on role (Head Coach, Assistant, Player).
Implement the volleyball court visualization using HTML/CSS Grid or an SVG overlay, ensuring 6 draggable/interactive positions for starting lineups.
Create two DataVolley modes: 1. Intermediate (large color-coded buttons for actions like Serve, Receive, Set, Attack, Block). 2. Advanced (text input or complex pro-style coding).
Use Recharts for post-match analytics (heat maps, bar charts for efficiency, line charts for momentum).
Follow the theme strictly: Light mode, high contrast, pure white surfaces against slate-50 backgrounds, with vibrant orange (#EA580C) and deep blue (#1E3A8A) accents.
Do not use generic Shadcn shadows. Use 1px borders instead for a premium, athletic aesthetic.
